title: SEC3: IDB opened fresh per operation instead of per session
description: storage.rs: `save_identity` and `load_identity` both call `open_db()` at the top, opening a new IdbDatabase handle on every call. The IndexedDB open is an async operation with an upgrade callback path. This is fine for the current two-function surface, but it is an under-engineered abstraction: any future caller that does multiple read/write operations will issue N open requests instead of one. The correct shape is to open the database once (at WASM init time or lazily on first use, stored in a `thread_local!` RefCell) and pass the handle to each operation. As it stands, adding a third storage function is a trap — the developer will copy the pattern and the problem silently multiplies.
